title: Fixing Missing Left Child Node Handling in a Binary Tree

statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.inputFormat: A binary tree represented as an array of node values, where each node value represents the value of a node, and empty values represent null children.
statement.outputFormat: The height of the binary tree after inserting a new root node.
statement.notes: The height of the tree is the number of nodes along the longest path from the root node to a leaf node.

hints.text: Check if the given node index is within bounds before accessing the array.

functionSignature.cpp: int heightOfTree(int* tree, int n, int root);
functionSignature.java: public int heightOfTree(int[] tree, int n, int root);
functionSignature.python: def heightOfTree(self, tree: list[int], n: int, root: int) -> int;
functionSignature.javascript: int heightOfTree(int tree[], int n, int root);

testCases.input: First line count, second line elements (stdin match)
